metadata.title: Master Protocols: Efficient Clinical Trial Design Strategies to Expedite Development of Oncology Drugs and Biologics: Guidance for Industry

rules.rule_text: The recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) should have been established for an investigational drug or drugs evaluated in a master protocol before proceeding with efficacy evaluation.
rules.source_section: Section I, Introduction
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, the recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) should have been established for an investigational drug or drugs evaluated in a master protocol.
rules.exceptions: Individual drug substudies under the master protocol might incorporate an initial dose-finding phase, for example, in pediatric subjects when sufficient adult data are available to inform a starting dose.

rules.rule_text: Master protocols must be well designed and well conducted to help ensure human subject safety and to generate data that meets regulatory standards for demonstrating each investigational drug's safety and effectiveness.
rules.source_section: Section II, Background
rules.verbatim_quote: Because of the complexity of these trials, which evaluate multiple drugs and/or disease populations, and their intent to support regulatory approval, it is important that such trials be well designed and well conducted to help ensure human subject safety and to generate data that meets regulatory standards for demonstrating each investigational drug's safety and effectiveness.

rules.rule_text: Each substudy within a basket trial must include specific objectives, the scientific rationale for inclusion of each population, and a detailed statistical analysis plan (SAP) that includes sample size justification and stopping rules for futility or efficacy.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A, Single Investigational Drug or Investigational Drug Combination Across Multiple Cancer Populations
rules.verbatim_quote: Each substudy should include specific objectives, the scientific rationale for inclusion of each population, and a detailed statistical analysis plan (SAP) that includes sample size justification and stopping rules for futility or efficacy.

rules.rule_text: FDA recommends that a sponsor use a common control arm to improve efficiency in master protocols where multiple drugs are evaluated simultaneously in a single disease; the control arm must be the currently recommended standard of care (SOC).
rules.source_section: Section V.A, Use of a Single Common Control Arm
rules.verbatim_quote: For oncology clinical trials, FDA recommends that a sponsor use a common control arm to improve efficiency in master protocols where multiple drugs are evaluated simultaneously in a single disease (e.g., umbrella trials). FDA recommends that the control arm be the current SOC so that the trial results will be interpretable in the context of U.S. medical practice.

rules.rule_text: When the SOC changes during the conduct of a master protocol trial, the sponsor must suspend subject enrollment until the protocol and the protocol informed consent document are modified to include the new SOC as control.
rules.source_section: Section V.A, Use of a Single Common Control Arm
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in SOC for the target population can occur during the conduct of the trial because of a new drug approval or new scientific evidence, making it no longer ethical to randomize human subjects to the previous SOC. In that case, the sponsor should suspend subject enrollment until the protocol and the protocol informed consent document are modified to include the new SOC as control.

rules.rule_text: The SAP for a master protocol must be modified as soon as possible and before any analysis of the data when the control arm changes.
rules.source_section: Section V.A, Use of a Single Common Control Arm
rules.verbatim_quote: The SAP should be modified as soon as possible and before any analysis of the data.

rules.rule_text: In master protocols with substudies evaluating concomitant administration of two or more investigational drugs, the sponsor must provide strong scientific rationale for the drug combination regimen and the RP2D must be identified for each individual drug.
rules.source_section: Section V.B, Novel Combination of Two or More Investigational Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: In master protocols with substudies intended to evaluate concomitant administration of two or more investigational drugs, the sponsor should provide strong scientific rationale for the use of the drug combination regimen. FDA recommends that the sponsor ensures the RP2D has been identified for each individual drug in all cases where each drug may have antitumor activity.
cross_references: Codevelopment of Two or More New Investigational Drugs for Use in Combination

rules.rule_text: Safety data from a minimum of six subjects treated at the proposed dosage for the drug combination regimen must be available before proceeding with the efficacy evaluation for novel combinations.
rules.source_section: Section V.B, Novel Combination of Two or More Investigational Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety data from a minimum of six subjects treated at the proposed dosage for the drug combination regimen should be available before proceeding with the efficacy evaluation.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor must submit results of the dose-finding phase to the IND for the master protocol before proceeding with the efficacy phase.
rules.source_section: Section V.B, Novel Combination of Two or More Investigational Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor should submit results of the dose-finding phase to the IND for the master protocol before proceeding with the efficacy phase.

rules.rule_text: For pediatric populations in master protocols, the full relevant age range of pediatric subjects (generally 0 to 17 years) must be addressed and the investigational drug must provide the prospect of direct clinical benefit.
rules.source_section: Section V.B, Novel Combination of Two or More Investigational Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: If such an approach is considered in a pediatric population, the sponsor should ensure, for a particular tumor type and/or relevant mutations, that the full relevant age range of pediatric subjects (generally speaking 0 to ≤17 years of age) is addressed and the investigational drug provides the prospect of direct clinical benefit to pediatric subjects.
rules.exceptions: Specific age eligibility should depend on the prevalence of the particular tumor type in children.

rules.rule_text: For master protocols with drugs targeting multiple biomarkers, subject selection tests must be analytically validated with well-defined criteria for marker positivity before initiation of the trial.
rules.source_section: Section V.C, Studies With Drugs Targeting Multiple Biomarkers
rules.verbatim_quote: For master protocols with drugs targeting multiple biomarkers, it is important that subject selection tests be analytically validated with well-defined criteria for marker positivity before initiation of the trial.

rules.rule_text: Protocols with IVD tests that are not analytically validated can be placed on clinical hold for deficiencies in design to meet the stated objectives.
rules.source_section: Section VI, Biomarker Development Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Use of IVDs with inadequate analytical performance characteristics (e.g., precision, accuracy) may produce unreliable results with respect to performance of the drug. Protocols with IVD tests that are not analytically validated can be placed on clinical hold for deficiencies in design to meet the stated objectives (21CFR 312.42(b)(2)(ii)).

rules.rule_text: Clinical investigations of devices that pose a significant risk generally require both FDA and IRB approval before initiation; FDA approval is obtained through submitting an investigational device exemption (IDE) application.
rules.source_section: Section VI, Biomarker Development Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical investigations of devices that pose a significant risk generally require both FDA and IRB approval before initiation. FDA approval can be obtained through submitting an investigational device exemption (IDE) application to FDA (§ 812.20).
rules.exceptions: Non-significant risk devices and exempted device investigations have different requirements.

rules.rule_text: Before initiating the trial, the sponsor must ensure that the master protocol and its associated SAP describe conditions that would result in adaptations such as sample size reestimation or discontinuation of an experimental arm based on futility rules.
rules.source_section: Section V.D, Adding and Stopping Treatment Arms
rules.verbatim_quote: Before initiating the trial, the sponsor should ensure that the master protocol and its associated SAP describe conditions that would result in adaptations such as the reestimation of the sample size based on the results of an interim analysis or discontinuation of an experimental arm based on futility rules.
cross_references: Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials of Drugs and Biologics

rules.rule_text: If results from one or more substudies are anticipated to form the basis of a marketing application, the master protocol must describe and provide the charter for an independent radiologic review committee and an independent data monitoring committee (IDMC).
rules.source_section: Section V.E, Independent Data Monitoring Committees
rules.verbatim_quote: If results from one or more substudies are anticipated to form the basis of a marketing application, the master protocol should describe and provide the charter for an independent radiologic review committee to perform blinded tumor-based assessments, as appropriate. In addition, the protocol should describe and provide a charter for an independent data monitoring committee (IDMC) or other appropriate independent entity to monitor efficacy and safety results.
cross_references: Establishment and Operation of Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees

rules.rule_text: The IDMC charter must authorize the committee to conduct prespecified and ad hoc assessments of efficacy and futility and recommend protocol modifications, including sample size adjustment and discontinuation or modification of a substudy based on futility or overwhelming evidence of efficacy.
rules.source_section: Section V.E, Independent Data Monitoring Committees
rules.verbatim_quote: The IDMC charter should authorize the committee to conduct prespecified and ad hoc assessments of efficacy and futility and recommend protocol modifications or other actions, including sample size adjustment and discontinuation or modification of a substudy based on futility or overwhelming evidence of efficacy.
cross_references: Establishment and Operation of Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees

rules.rule_text: In nonrandomized protocols where the primary endpoint is overall response rate, the planned sample size must be sufficient to rule out a clinically unimportant response rate based on the lower bound of the 95 percent confidence interval around the observed response rate.
rules.source_section: Section VII.A, Nonrandomized, Activity-Estimating Design
rules.verbatim_quote: In nonrandomized protocols, where the primary endpoint is overall response rate, the planned sample size should be sufficient to rule out a clinically unimportant response rate based on the lower bound of the 95 percent confidence interval around the observed response rate.

rules.rule_text: For master protocols employing adaptive/Bayesian designs, the SAP must provide all information described in the FDA guidances for Adaptive Designs and Enrichment Strategies, must describe plans for futility analyses, and must include details on implementation of Bayesian or other methods.
rules.source_section: Section VII.C, Master Protocols Employing Adaptive/Bayesian Design
rules.verbatim_quote: In master protocols that incorporate adaptive designs, the SAP should provide all information described in the guidances for industry Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials of Drugs and Biologics and Enrichment Strategies for Clinical Trials to Support Determination of Effectiveness of Human Drugs and Biological Products. The SAP should also describe plans for futility analyses. ... The SAP should include details on implementation of Bayesian or other methods.
cross_references: Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials of Drugs and Biologics

rules.rule_text: The sponsor of the master protocol IND is required to ensure proper monitoring of the investigations and conduct in accordance with the general investigational plan and protocols contained in the IND.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.A, Safety Monitoring and Reporting Plans
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor of the master protocol IND is required to ensure proper monitoring of the investigations and to ensure that the investigations are conducted in accordance with the general investigational plan and protocols contained in the IND (21 CFR 312.50).

rules.rule_text: The master protocol IND sponsor must establish a systematic approach that ensures rapid communication of serious safety issues to all participating clinical investigators and FDA under IND safety reporting regulations.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.A, Safety Monitoring and Reporting Plans
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor of the master protocol IND should establish a systematic approach that ensures rapid communication of serious safety issues to all participating clinical investigators and FDA under IND safety reporting regulations.

rules.rule_text: The master protocol IND sponsor must ensure rapid communication of serious safety issues to the sponsors of all products used in the master protocol, according to the established safety reporting plan, and must describe the process for rapid implementation of protocol amendments to address serious safety issues.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.A, Safety Monitoring and Reporting Plans
rules.verbatim_quote: Additionally the master protocol IND sponsor should ensure rapid communication of serious safety issues to the sponsors of all products used in the master protocol, according to the established safety reporting plan. In addition, the approach should describe the process for rapid implementation of protocol amendments to address serious safety issues.

rules.rule_text: For all master protocols, the sponsor must institute an IDMC or other appropriate independent entity to assess safety in addition to efficacy; the sponsor must describe in the IND the constitution of this committee and the definition of its responsibilities.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.B, Independent Safety Assessment
rules.verbatim_quote: For all master protocols, the sponsor should institute an IDMC or other appropriate independent entity structured to assess safety in addition to efficacy (section V. E., Independent Data Monitoring Committee). The sponsor should describe in the IND the constitution of this committee and the definition of its responsibilities.
cross_references: Establishment and Operation of Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees

rules.rule_text: The IDMC must complete real-time review of all serious adverse events as defined in FDA regulations and periodically assess the totality of safety information in the development program.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.B, Independent Safety Assessment
rules.verbatim_quote: The committee should complete the real-time review of all serious adverse events as defined in FDA regulations and periodically assess the totality of safety information in the development program.
cross_references: Establishment and Operation of Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees

rules.rule_text: A clinical trial being conducted under a master protocol IND must not be initiated until an IRB or independent ethics committee has reviewed and approved the protocol, and the trial remains subject to continuing review by an IRB.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.C, Institutional Review Board/Independent Ethics Committee
rules.verbatim_quote: A clinical trial being conducted under a master protocol IND must not be initiated until an IRB or independent ethics committee has reviewed and approved the protocol, and the trial remains subject to continuing review by an IRB.
rules.exceptions: Protocol amendments necessary to eliminate apparent immediate hazards to trial participants can generally be immediately implemented but are required to be reported to the IRB afterward.

rules.rule_text: Modifications to the master protocol must be approved by the independent ethics committee before implementation, except for amendments necessary to eliminate apparent immediate hazards to trial participants.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.C, Institutional Review Board/Independent Ethics Committee
rules.verbatim_quote: After initiation, modifications to the master protocol must be approved by the independent ethics committee before implementation, with the exception of protocol amendments that are necessary to eliminate apparent immediate hazards to trial participants, which can generally be immediately implemented but are required to be reported to the IRB afterward.
rules.exceptions: Amendments necessary to eliminate apparent immediate hazards can be immediately implemented but must be reported to the IRB afterward.

rules.rule_text: To facilitate IRB review of master protocols, FDA recommends the use of a central IRB that has adequate resources and appropriate expertise to review master protocols in a timely and thorough manner.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.C, Institutional Review Board/Independent Ethics Committee
rules.verbatim_quote: To facilitate IRB review of master protocols, FDA recommends the use of a central IRB. The central IRB should have adequate resources and appropriate expertise to review master protocols in a timely and thorough manner.
cross_references: Using a Centralized IRB Review Process in Multicenter Clinical Trials

rules.rule_text: When a master protocol includes plans to enroll pediatric subjects, FDA recommends the IRB include an individual or individuals who have expertise in the management of pediatric oncology patients.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.C, Institutional Review Board/Independent Ethics Committee
rules.verbatim_quote: Irrespective of the type of IRB that is used, if the master protocol includes plans to enroll pediatric subjects in the trial, FDA recommends the IRB include (either as a member or an invited nonvoting expert) an individual or individuals who have expertise in the management of pediatric oncology patients.

rules.rule_text: In addition to submitting informed consent documents to the IRB, the sponsor may need to submit the original and all updated informed consent documents to the IND to allow FDA to assess that human subjects have the information to make informed decisions.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.D, Informed Consent Document
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to submitting informed consent documents to the IRB for review, the sponsor may need to submit the original and all updated informed consent documents to the IND to allow FDA to assess that human subjects have the information to make informed decisions about participation in the trial (21 CFR 50.20).

rules.rule_text: Protocol amendments submitted under 21 CFR 312.30 should be accompanied by the revised informed consent documents unless immediate modifications are intended to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to subjects.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.D, Informed Consent Document
rules.verbatim_quote: Protocol amendments submitted under 21 CFR 312.30 should be accompanied by the revised informed consent documents unless immediate modifications are intended to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to subjects, in which case the sponsor should submit the revised informed consent document as soon as possible.
rules.exceptions: If immediate modifications are intended to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to subjects, the revised informed consent document should be submitted as soon as possible.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must submit each master protocol as part of a new IND to FDA; the master protocol must be the only trial conducted under that IND.
rules.source_section: Section IX, Additional Regulatory Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: Because of the complexity of master protocols and the need to avoid miscommunication that could compromise subject safety, sponsors should submit each master protocol as part of a new IND to FDA. For INDs that contain master protocols, sponsors should consider the following: The master protocol should be the only trial that is conducted under the IND.

rules.rule_text: INDs containing master protocols are subject to all the requirements under 21 CFR 312 and must contain the required elements for clinical protocols described in 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6)(iii); submission must be in eCTD format.
rules.source_section: Section X.A, New IND Submission
rules.verbatim_quote: INDs containing master protocols are subject to all the requirements under 21 CFR 312. To ensure that all required aspects are complete, a master protocol must contain the required elements for clinical protocols described in 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6)(iii) and should contain all the information described in sections V, VI, VII, and VIII of this guidance. Specifically, the protocol and IND submission must address the following elements: (1) Core elements as required per 21 CFR 312.20-23 (2) Submission in electronic (electronic common technical document (eCTD)) format.

rules.rule_text: The master protocol must also include a detailed description of the trial design as text and as a visual depiction; procedures for biomarker sample acquisition, handling, and testing; prominent identification of all substudies; and description of all groups responsible for monitoring subject safety.
rules.source_section: Section X.A, New IND Submission
rules.verbatim_quote: The master protocol should also include the following: (1) A detailed description of the trial design as text and as a visual depiction (2) Procedures for sample acquisition, handling, and testing of biomarkers, as well as information pertaining to any IVDs employed in the trial, as appropriate (3) Prominent identification of all substudies (4) Description of all groups responsible for monitoring subject safety (e.g., IRB, IDMC).

rules.rule_text: Protocol amendments that substantively affect the safety or scope of the master protocol must contain an updated informed consent document, a clean and tracked changes version of the amended master protocol, and a tabular list of proposed changes with rationale.
rules.source_section: Section X.B, Amendments to the Master Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Protocol amendments that substantively affect the safety or scope of the master protocol should contain the following: (1) An updated informed consent document (2) A clean and tracked changes version of the amended master protocol document (3) A list of the proposed changes in tabular format with the rationale for each proposed change and the following supportive information, if available: (a) Summary of available safety and efficacy data (b) New nonclinical toxicology or pharmacology data and clinical data as appropriate to support the protocol modification.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must notify the regulatory project manager via secure email or a phone call 48 hours before submitting any protocol amendment that substantively affects the safety or scope of the protocol.
rules.source_section: Section XI, Communication and Interactions with FDA
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor should notify the regulatory project manager via secure email or a phone call 48 hours before submitting any protocol amendment that substantively affects the safety or scope of the protocol.

rules.rule_text: If a master protocol amendment contains changes intended to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to subjects, the sponsor must implement immediately the revised protocol; the sponsor must ensure FDA is subsequently notified by protocol amendment and the reviewing IRB is notified.
rules.source_section: Section XI, Communication and Interactions with FDA
rules.verbatim_quote: If the amendment contains changes intended to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to subjects (e.g., closure of a substudy for unacceptable toxicity, modification of eligibility or monitoring to mitigate the risks), the sponsor should implement immediately the revised protocol. The sponsor must ensure that FDA is subsequently notified by protocol amendment and the reviewing IRB is notified in accordance with 21 CFR 56.104(c).

rules.rule_text: For other substantive changes that affect safety, scope, or the scientific quality of the study, the revised protocol must not be initiated until 30 days after submission to the IND to allow FDA to assess the risks, and until the change has been approved by the IRB.
rules.source_section: Section XI, Communication and Interactions with FDA
rules.verbatim_quote: For other substantive changes that affect safety, scope, or the scientific quality of the study, the cover letter should contain a statement that the revised protocol will not be initiated until 30 days after submission to the IND to allow FDA to assess the risks of the proposed change and until the change has been approved by the IRB.

rules.rule_text: For clinical development programs evaluating combinations of two or more investigational drugs, the general investigational plan must describe the approach to demonstrating the contribution of each investigational drug to the observed treatment effect to support a benefit-risk assessment.
rules.source_section: Section V.B, Novel Combination of Two or More Investigational Drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: For clinical development programs designed to evaluate combinations of two or more investigational drugs, it is important that the general investigational plan describe the approach to demonstrating the contribution of each investigational drug to the observed treatment effect to support a benefit-risk assessment.
cross_references: Codevelopment of Two or More New Investigational Drugs for Use in Combination
